response: {"purpose":"Figure 1d displays RBFOX2 RNA binding protein read density (in reads per million usable, RPM) across a genomic region, comparing iCLIP, two biological replicates of eCLIP with their paired size-matched input (SMInput) controls, and IgG-only controls. It aims to demonstrate that eCLIP produces reproducible, specific signal enriched above background, as evidenced by CLIPper-identified binding site clusters (shown as boxes), with dark-colored boxes specifically marking sites significantly enriched over the SMInput. This comparison matters because it directly illustrates the advantage of the SMInput normalization strategy in distinguishing genuine RBP binding sites from non-specific or background signal, a key methodological improvement of eCLIP over iCLIP.","title":"RBFOX2 Read Density Comparing eCLIP, iCLIP, and Control Samples"}
